title: Spectralize
description: Reinterpret zeros, gaps, stability or obstructions as eigenvalues, spectra or trace data.
output: candidate operator, spectral gap statement or trace identity
risks: The operator may not be self-adjoint.
Spectral data may be only analogy.
lean_test: Create a finite-dimensional matrix model and prove the toy spectral statement.

title: Categorify
description: Replace sets or equations by objects, morphisms, functors and natural transformations.
output: functorial dictionary or universal property
risks: The categorical lift may hide the original hard estimate.
lean_test: State the relevant universal property in a small category.

title: Geometrize
description: Translate algebraic, analytic or computational data into geometric objects and invariants.
output: variety, moduli space, bundle, curvature or geometric invariant
risks: The geometry may not preserve the invariant needed for the problem.
lean_test: Formalize the toy geometric object and one preserved invariant.

title: Probabilize
description: Use random models, typical cases, concentration or stochastic dynamics to expose structure.
output: random model, concentration claim or stochastic analogue
risks: Average behavior may erase worst-case or singular behavior.
lean_test: Build a finite probability model with explicit event bounds.

title: Renormalize
description: Track how quantities change across scales and search for fixed points or monotonicity.
output: scale flow, critical exponent, invariant or monotone quantity
risks: The flow may be heuristic without a rigorous topology.
lean_test: Prove a finite scale-recursion lemma.

title: Dualize
description: Reverse perspective using dual spaces, Pontryagin/Fourier duality, categorical duals or primal-dual optimization.
output: dual problem or transformed obstruction
risks: The dual problem can lose positivity, locality or computability.
lean_test: State the dual construction and prove involutivity in a toy case.

title: Local to global
description: Patch local data into global structure or identify the obstruction to gluing.
output: sheaf, descent condition, compactness argument or obstruction class
risks: Local solvability may not control global compatibility.
lean_test: Formalize a tiny gluing lemma or counterexample.

title: Deform
description: Study a family instead of a single object, then analyze limits, degenerations or stability.
output: family, parameter space, degeneration or stability invariant
risks: The special fiber may not preserve the desired property.
lean_test: Represent a finite family and prove a property is stable under a simple deformation.

title: Tropicalize
description: Pass to piecewise-linear or valuation shadows to expose combinatorial structure.
output: polyhedral or valuation model
risks: The shadow may be too coarse to recover the original invariant.
lean_test: Formalize a valuation-like toy map and preserved inequality.

title: Finitize
description: Replace a continuous or infinite object by a finite toy model that preserves one mechanism.
output: finite model, computable experiment or small Lean theorem
risks: Finite artifacts can create false structure.
lean_test: Prove the finite toy theorem completely.

title: Find the barrier
description: Identify why standard methods fail and encode the failure as an invariant or obstruction.
output: barrier statement, obstruction invariant or no-go lemma
risks: The named barrier may be sociological rather than mathematical.
lean_test: Formalize the simplest version of the obstruction.

title: Transfer by functor
description: Move a problem through a functor or equivalence while tracking which structure is preserved.
output: source category, target category, preserved structure and lost structure
risks: The functor may forget exactly the hard information.
lean_test: Define a small functor and prove preservation of one property.

title: Cancel before majorizing
description: Preserve signed, graded or symmetry-related contributions long enough to apply an exact or defective Ward identity before taking norms and triangle inequalities.
output: a decomposition into an exact sector, a quantified defect and a residual activity profile
risks: An abstract differential may not act on the actual measure or regulator.
Taking absolute values too early destroys the cancellation being tested.
lean_test: Prove the finite weighted Ward identity and derive a norm bound controlled only by the weight variation.

title: Separate support roles
description: Use distinct support maps for connectivity or incompatibility and for full target union or locality, then prove the comparison theorem explicitly.
output: two support maps, an active-target comparison and a target-fiber identity without lost multiplicity
risks: Collapsing the supports can create false factorization or erase multiple connected covers with the same target.
lean_test: Prove target-map injectivity only inside admissible families and retain a fiber sum for duplicate targets.

title: Rescale physical units
description: Separate dimensionless lattice quantities from physical units and track exact invariants before taking continuum or thermodynamic limits.
output: an exact unit-conversion identity plus explicit convergence and finite-volume obligations
risks: An arbitrarily chosen lattice spacing can manufacture a meaningless physical scale.
A decay-rate witness need not equal the optimal spectral mass.
lean_test: Prove the exponent identity, ceiling approximation error and block-scale invariance of the physical mass ratio.

title: Preserve the exact target
description: Delay global overcounting until the exact union fiber, target label and local relation have produced every conclusion that depends on them.
output: fixed-target summation with an extracted target weight and an explicit remaining local kernel
risks: The retained state can make finite reindexing and dependent bookkeeping substantially harder.
lean_test: Define a finite exact-union fiber and prove that metric extraction holds before, but need not hold after, forgetting the target.

title: Eliminate tree leaves
description: Orient a connected tree from a root and sum assignments from the leaves upward using parent-child kernel moments and accumulated child powers.
output: fixed-tree assignment bound with child-count factorials and a separately auditable tree-shape sum
risks: A global child budget can reintroduce volume dependence if incompatibility with the parent is not retained.
lean_test: Prove the bound for rooted trees with at most three vertices and then generalize by deleting a maximal-depth leaf.

title: Audit information loss
description: For every abstraction or majorant, list the target, locality, signs, edges and scale data it forgets, then test whether the desired conclusion still mentions them.
output: retained-versus-forgotten data table, circularity check and smallest finite counterexample
risks: Applied indiscriminately, the audit can reject coarse bounds that are valid after the relevant structure has already been consumed.
lean_test: Prove an exact factorization of the coarse object and exhibit two inputs with the same coarse data but different target-sensitive statistics.

title: Normalize by the parent kernel weight
description: Divide a parent-dependent child moment kernel by a strictly positive parent weight, then recover the denominator product exactly from rooted child multiplicities.
output: vertexwise rooted-walk budget with exact cancellation of parent metric denominators
risks: The normalization is invalid if the parent weight can vanish or if the parent-product identity does not match the chosen orientation.
lean_test: Check the cancellation identity for every rooted tree on at most four vertices before proving the general child-fiber product theorem.

title: Close the exact generating function
description: Replace a coarse coefficient bound by the exact combinatorial enumeration, then sum its known generating function and retain the resulting stability information.
output: closed square-root majorant, exact convergence boundary and sensitivity bound
risks: The exact enumeration may use a different rooting, labelling or tree class from the analytic coefficient actually present.
lean_test: Enumerate labelled rooted trees for n up to five and compare the normalized child-factorial sum with the Catalan number.

title: Condition on a separator
description: Insert a collar or separator, condition on its variables and test whether the remaining precision or graph decomposes into independent components.
output: exact conditional factorization or an explicit cross-block defect localized at the separator
risks: Projectors, gauge constraints or integrating out the separator may create dense effective couplings and destroy exact separation.
lean_test: Prove the three-block positive-definite Gaussian case and falsify the statement when a nonzero A-C precision block is inserted.

title: Require one uniform recovery map
description: Audit recovery claims by demanding one channel that works for every state in the code and preserves entanglement with an external reference.
output: quantifier-correct recovery theorem or a counterexample showing state-dependent preparation only
risks: Replacing existence for each state by a single uniform channel is a genuinely stronger obligation and may fail without a code or energy restriction.
lean_test: Construct a two-state finite code where separate state-preparation maps exist and test whether any single channel recovers both coherently.

title: Localize a homotopy and expose the boundary defect
description: Sandwich a global homotopy by a support projector and rewrite the failure of the contraction identity as commutators with that projector.
output: explicit boundary-supported defect operator with separately auditable locality and decay
risks: Graded signs, nonlocal homotopy kernels or a noncommuting projection can make the boundary term larger than the original remainder.
lean_test: Prove the ungraded matrix identity first, then add a finite cochain complex where the commutator support is computed explicitly.
